name: Anomalous Descent
theme: Science-fantasy megadungeon in far-future post-apocalyptic Earth
flavor_description:
Thousands of years after civilization collapsed, the city of Denethix clings to order at the edge of a wilderness teeming with dinosaurs, moktars, and rogue wizards. Beneath Mount Rendon lies the Anomalous Subsurface Environment — a self-spawning megadungeon that predates humanity, filled with ancient super-science relics, degraded automatons, and things far worse. Adventurers descend seeking fortune. Most don't return.

The Vizier, a former slave named Koyl Yrenum, rules Denethix through a web of lies — the city's founder Feretha is dead, his brain scooped out and hooked to a machine, but everyone must believe he still lives. The Unyielding Fist enforces order with ancient laser weapons. The Cult of Science worships technology they barely understand. The Society of the Luminous Spark fights slavery by extracting slavers' brains. And deep in the wilderness, the House of Flesh pulses with eldritch horror where Starry Wisdom cultists have transformed a forest mansion into a living sanctum of alien terror.

The tone is played straight — the gonzo humor lives in flavor text. Banks guarded by 20-foot robots with machine guns. A Head Scientist on 12-foot stilts. Wizards who fly around in giant stone heads that shoot lasers from their eyes. Clockwork cats and dolls made of human teeth. The world takes itself seriously; the absurdity speaks for itself.
special_constraints: Artifact subtheme — ancient technology, automatons, protonium-metal relics
One planeswalker only: Jace (visiting from another plane, investigating the ASE)
Include dinosaurs as creature type (canon to setting)
House of Flesh as a card cycle (legendary enchantment/land + cultist creatures)
Deeper dungeon levels hinted in flavor text but not depicted

legendary_characters.name: Feretha, the Undying
legendary_characters.role: Dead wizard-ruler hooked to a machine, brain in a computer. Used as puppet to deter rival wizards.

legendary_characters.name: Koyl Yrenum, the Vizier
legendary_characters.role: Former slave who became de facto ruler of Denethix. Maintains the lie that Feretha lives. Obsessed with city planning.

legendary_characters.name: Captain Marcus Tyro
legendary_characters.role: Captain of the Unyielding Fist. Co-conspirator in the Feretha cover-up. Pragmatic military leader.

legendary_characters.name: Fereyn, Head in the Clouds
legendary_characters.role: Rival wizard who flies around in a giant detachable stone head with eye lasers. Sends Exterminators to raid.

legendary_characters.name: Monsator, the Reaper
legendary_characters.role: Wizard who commands Cornstalk Warriors from horseback (his draft horse Tess). Controls nearby farmland.

legendary_characters.name: The Head Scientist
legendary_characters.role: Leader of the Cult of Science. Towers above crowds on stilts, 12-foot lab coat, prosthetic limbs. Worships ancient tech.

legendary_characters.name: Terrible Karak
legendary_characters.role: Bandit king of Chelmsfordshire. Plots robbery and murder when 'feeling frisky'.

legendary_characters.name: Jace, Anomaly Seeker
legendary_characters.role: Planeswalker visiting from another plane, drawn by the ASE's anomalous magical signature. Investigating the dungeon's mysteries.

notable_cards.name: Fereyn's Stone Head
notable_cards.notes: Detachable flying vehicle that shoots eye lasers. Crew ability to detach from a creature.

notable_cards.name: The Anomalous Subsurface Environment
notable_cards.notes: The dungeon itself — 'will without intelligence, embodied in stone'. Taps for colorless, activated abilities representing dungeon exploration.

notable_cards.name: Bank Inviolable
notable_cards.notes: Protected by Custodians of Fiduciary Duty — 20-foot iron-and-gold robots with machine guns.

notable_cards.name: House of Flesh
notable_cards.notes: Starry Wisdom cultist sanctum. Forest mansion transformed into living eldritch horror. Mysterious purpose.

notable_cards.name: God's Eye
notable_cards.notes: Giant black metal circle with a slitted purple eye. Touching it can compel violence.

draft_archetypes.name: Ancient Technology
draft_archetypes.description: Control the board with automatons, protonium relics, and ancient machinery. Use artifacts for card advantage and incremental value.

draft_archetypes.name: Vizier's Regime
draft_archetypes.description: Authoritarian control through sacrifice and taxation. Drain opponents while building an army of loyal subjects. The Vizier's lie holds the city together.

draft_archetypes.name: Spark Rebellion
draft_archetypes.description: Aggressive freedom fighters who punch through defenses. Go-wide with rebels and use equipment/weapons to overwhelm. Combat tricks and team buffs.
draft_archetypes.faction: Society of the Luminous Spark, Captain Tyro

draft_archetypes.name: Frontier Settlers
draft_archetypes.description: Build a board of settlers, farmers, and domesticated beasts. Token generation, +1/+1 counters, and go-wide strategies from the villages around Denethix.

draft_archetypes.name: Deep Descent
draft_archetypes.description: Delve into the ASE for forbidden knowledge. Mill, graveyard interaction, and card selection. Each descent deeper yields greater power — and greater risk.

draft_archetypes.name: Mad Science
draft_archetypes.description: Cast instants and sorceries fueled by poorly understood ancient tech. Spellslinger with artifact synergies. The Cult of Science's experiments gone right (and wrong).

draft_archetypes.name: Dungeon Ecology
draft_archetypes.description: The dungeon's self-maintaining ecosystem: fungal growth, dungeon elementals, and creatures adapted to the deep. +1/+1 counters and creature-based card advantage.

draft_archetypes.name: Raider Warbands
draft_archetypes.description: Aggressive moktar warbands and bandits that sacrifice resources for explosive damage. Attack with reckless abandon, use hypno-weapons, burn everything.

draft_archetypes.name: Twisted Evolution
draft_archetypes.description: Creatures warped by the dungeon's influence and Starry Wisdom corruption. Graveyard recursion, death triggers, and creatures that grow stronger as things die.

draft_archetypes.name: Prehistoric Fury
draft_archetypes.description: Dinosaurs, giant beasts, and the raw power of the untamed wilderness. Ramp into massive creatures and trample over opposition.

flavor_text_guidelines.tone: Deadpan, darkly humorous. The world takes itself seriously; the absurdity speaks for itself.
themes: Bureaucratic absurdity (banks with robot guards, city planning obsession)
The lie that holds Denethix together
The dungeon as a living, self-aware entity
sample_quotes: The Custodians of Fiduciary Duty occasionally fire on individuals waiting in line, reducing them to a fine red mist. The Bank considers this an acceptable default rate.
The Head Scientist's 12-foot lab coat is not merely ostentatious. It also conceals the stilts.
The screams from Level 3 have stopped. That's worse.
Feretha's decrees have become notably more coherent since his death.
The dungeon has been accident-free for three thousand, seven hundred, twenty-nine years, eleven months, and two days. A company record!
